caveat: NOTE: This is a statistical cross-check, not a replacement for interpretive coding. Topic models produce word clusters, not themes.

results.text: I think there's three elements to that. The first is provenance, right? And we've seen a lot of folks trying to innovate different ways to provide watermarks or tags, things that verify that something you're seeing is a real piece of information versus a deep fake. What we haven't seen is some industry consolidation around agreeing to some common standards and some common benchmarks and some common frames that let us think about provenance not just between one user and one provider, but across the entire ecosystem of information. There's a real opportunity there for us to figure out how do we actually tag something to be true when it is. The second element of it is really thinking about verification models. And here AI presents a real opportunity that I think is under-discussed. When somebody has a piece of information, they're trying to ask the question, is this real or not?
